items.title: Puteri Umno chief urges stronger support for M40 women, says group often overlooked
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Puteri Umno chief Datuk Nurul Amal Mohd Fauzi today urged the government to strengthen support for young women in the middle-income (M40) group, warning that many remain overlooked despite facing mounting economic and social pressures. “This group is not merely carrying the burden of daily living, but is also balancing responsibilities and expectations,” she said. She also called on the government to move beyond short-term assistance and instead develop a more comprehensive ecosystem that would enable young women to thrive.

items.title: Eight relief centres for Kampung Bahagia fire evacuees close
items.summary: SANDAKAN: Eight temporary relief centres housing evacuees from the Kampung Bahagia fire here were closed today. District disaster management committee chairman Datuk Walter Kenson said the decision was made following an agreement reached at a special meeting on the fire disaster held yesterday. "The closure, effective 1pm today, involves centres at the Taman Batu Sapi People's Housing Project Hall, Sekolah Kebangsaan Kampung Gas, Sekolah Kebangsaan Kampung Bahagia, Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Batu Sapi, Dewan Serbaguna Sibuga, Dewan Serbaguna Warga Tua and the Sim-Sim futsal court," he said in a statement.

items.title: Kota Bharu college offers support to family of teen student murdered in Ketereh, pledges full cooperation in police investigation
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 2 — Kolej Poly-Tech Mara Sdn Bhd (KPTMSB) has expressed sorrow over the death of its 19-year-old student from its campus here who was found in Ketereh, Kelantan with multiple stab wounds yesterday. The college said it is fully cooperating with the police amid the murder investigations. Nurfisya Zulkifly was discovered dead by the roadside near a paddy field in Kampung Simah in Ketereh with 61 stab wounds on May 1, 2026, Kelantan police said.

items.title: Puteri Umno invites Syed Saddiq, Bella Astillah to join Rumah Bangsa
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Puteri Umno has extended an invitation to Muar member of parliament Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman and his fiancée, singer Bella Astillah, to join the party's Rumah Bangsa initiative. Puteri Umno chief Datuk Nurul Amal Mohd Fauzi said Rumah Bangsa, initiated under Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi's leadership, aims to foster reconciliation, openness and unity. Speaking at Umno's 80th anniversary celebrations at the World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur here today, Nurul Amal said Rumah Bangsa is open to all without favouritism.

items.title: Woman jumps from moving e-hailing car in Klang over safety fears
items.summary: North Klang police chief Assistant Commissioner S. Vijaya Rao said the incident occurred about 9am and was reported by a 47-year-old local e-hailing driver. He said the driver immediately stopped the car and found that the woman had sustained minor injuries. Vijaya Rao said the driver later lodged a police report as a precautionary measure.

items.title: Starmer says some pro-Palestinian marches could be banned
items.summary: LONDON: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has said banning some pro-Palestinian marches could be justified, particularly where chants call for the spread of the "intifada". Starmer is facing pressure to act following a series of antisemitic incidents, including a stabbing this week in Golders Green, north London, an area with a large Jewish community. Police said they would closely assess all future protest-related incidents.

items.title: Iran executes two men convicted of spying for Israel
items.summary: TEHRAN: Iran hanged two men on Saturday convicted of spying for Israel, the judiciary said. The judiciary's Mizan Online website said Yaghoub Karimpour and Nasser Bekrzadeh were hanged for "intelligence cooperation and espionage in favour of the Zionist regime". Mizan said Karimpour was convicted of the capital offence of "moharebeh," or waging war against God, for filming and photographing security and military locations.

items.title: Humpback whale stranded in Germany released into North Sea
items.summary: FRANKFURT: A humpback whale that had been struggling to survive after beaching near the German coast was today released into the North Sea off Denmark after being transported in a barge, a member of a rescue mission said. Dubbed "Timmy" by the German media, the whale was first spotted stuck on a sandbank on March 23 near the city of Luebeck before freeing itself and then becoming stuck again several times. The whale left the barge it had been towed on from Wismar Bay on the Baltic coast at around 8:45am (0645 GMT), said Karin Walter-Mommert from the rescue initiative.

items.title: MetMalaysia warns of afternoon thunderstorms in all states, heavy rain and strong winds in Klang Valley
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — The Meteorological Department has issued a thunderstorm warning covering the Klang Valley and several other states, with heavy rain and strong winds expected until 6pm today. Affected areas include Selangor, Kuala Lumpur, and Putrajaya, as well as multiple regions across Perlis, Kedah, Penang and Perak. The alert also covers Labuan, with thunderstorms expected across all listed areas.

items.title: Umno proposes 'Rumah Generasi Baru' initiative to win over young voters
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Umno is looking to institutionalise youth engagement ahead of upcoming elections, with Melaka Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh proposing the establishment of a "Rumah Generasi Baru" under the party's broader Rumah Bangsa framework. Speaking at Umno's 80th anniversary convention at the World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur, Rauf said the initiative is aimed at ensuring leadership continuity and strengthening the party's long-term support base. Rauf said the shift has disrupted the party's traditional grassroots intelligence systems, limiting its ability to effectively track and engage new voters.

items.title: Iran announces new maritime rules amid Strait of Hormuz tensions
items.summary: ISTANBUL: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has announced "new rules" governing the country's coastline along the Gulf and the Strait of Hormuz, amid heightened regional tensions. US President Donald Trump later unilaterally extended the truce without setting a new timeframe, at Pakistan's request. –BERNAMA-ANADOLU © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Heavy rain, strong winds expected as MetMalaysia issues nationwide alert
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning for multiple states, with heavy rain and strong winds expected to persist until 6pm today. In a statement issued at 3.35pm, MetMalaysia said the warning covers Perlis, Kedah and Penang, as well as several districts in Perak, including Kerian, Larut, Matang dan Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar, Bagan Datuk, Hilir Perak, Batang Padang and Muallim. The department said similar conditions are forecast in Kelantan, involving Pasir Mas, Jeli, Tanah Merah, Machang, Kuala Krai and Gua Musang, as well as Terengganu, covering Besut, Setiu, Hulu Terengganu, Dungun and Kemaman.

items.title: Iran military official says renewed war with US 'likely'
items.summary: TEHRAN: A senior Iranian military official has said renewed fighting between Iran and the United States is "likely", amid renewed tension over stalled nuclear negotiations. Trump said he was unhappy with progress in talks, citing "tremendous discord" within Iran's leadership, and suggested military action remained an option, though he said he would "prefer not" to take it. "A renewed conflict between Iran and the United States is likely," said Mohammad Jafar Asadi, a senior figure in Iran's central military command, according to the Fars news agency.

items.title: Trump says 'not satisfied' with new Iran proposal
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump said Friday he was "not satisfied" with a new Iranian negotiating proposal, as peace talks remain stalled despite a weeks-long ceasefire. he said, adding he would "prefer not" to take military action "on a human basis". Trump, under domestic pressure over congressional authorisation, wrote to lawmakers declaring hostilities "terminated", despite no change in US military posture.

items.title: Children seen sticking heads out of sunroof in viral video sparks concern
items.summary: Two children stood inside the vehicle with their heads protruding through the sunroof. The post carried a caption urging parents and road users to be responsible and not endanger children's lives. "Be responsible parents and road users.

items.title: Kicked, dragged, dunked in water: Four teen boys under police questioning after report of bullying at Tanjung Kling orphanage
items.summary: MELAKA, May 2 — Police detained four male students to assist in the investigation into a bullying case involving a resident at an orphanage in Tanjung Kling, here. Melaka Police Chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said police received a report on April 29 at about 7.11 pm from the victim’s mother, who alleged that her son, a student residing at Rumah Anak Yatim Sultan Salahuddin Abdul Aziz Shah Al Haj Pantai Puteri, had been bullied by senior students known to him. Dzulkhairi said all four suspects were released on police bail after their statements were recorded to assist further investigation.

items.title: Police chase on NKVE ends with Proton Preve ramming BMW in Subang, fleeing driver has criminal and drug records
items.summary: Petaling Jaya police chief ACP Shamsudin Mamat said a search of the suspects’ car also revealed 17 transparent packets containing suspected drugs, weighing around 16 grammes. “The 45-year-old male suspect drove dangerously, entered the Subang Jaya intersection, and crashed into a black BMW at the traffic lights on Persiaran Tujuan in Subang Jaya, before police finally managed to apprehend him at the scene,” Shamsudin said in a statement today. “The male suspect tested positive for methamphetamine.

items.title: Wild elephants destroy pineapple farm in Jerantut causing RM20k losses
items.summary: JERANTUT: A pineapple farmer suffered losses of nearly RM20,000 after his farm was damaged by a herd of wild elephants at the Lembah Gelanggi Agricultural Project in Pulau Tawar near here today. Zainul Ismail, 68, said nearly 50 MD2 pineapple plants were damaged in the incident, believed to have occurred between 3am and 6am. "The 20ha farm has about 100,000 pineapple plants and part of the harvest has already been marketed," he said.

items.title: Rain Rave festival debate should not be framed in extremes, says Johor DAP vice-chairman
items.summary: KUCHING, May 2 — The debate over the Rain Rave Water Music Festival 2026 should not be reduced to extreme positions and must instead be approached rationally, balancing values, freedom and economic considerations, according to Johor DAP vice-chairman Sheikh Umar Bagharib Ali. In a statement posted on Facebook, Sheikh Umar said Malaysia’s position as a Muslim-majority country with a diverse society requires policy discussions to be handled in a measured manner rather than emotionally. Sheikh Umar also said Malaysia must mature in its understanding of majority rule and minority rights, stressing that while the majority shapes national direction, minority rights must still be protected.

items.title: SME group hails Rain Rave festival as key economic driver
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Rain Rave 2026 Water Music Festival is a timely boost for small and medium enterprises amid mounting economic pressures, the SME Association of Malaysia said. The Rain Rave 2026 Water Music Festival was held in Bukit Bintang, Kuala Lumpur from April 30 to May 2, 2026. Chin said Bukit Bintang, one of the country's main tourism and commercial hubs, provides an ideal platform to showcase Malaysia's diversity, creativity and hospitality.

items.title: Boy killed in 3-vehicle crash in Kampar, Hilux driver arrested for alleged drug use
items.summary: Police said the Hilux driver tested positive for cannabis after the collision at KM29.4 of Jalan Ipoh–Kuala Lumpur. District police chief Superintendent Mohamad Nazri Daud said the incident occurred at 10.35pm after police received a public alert on a collision involving the three vehicles. He said the Myvi driver, 23, and the Hilux driver, 44, were not injured.

items.title: Sabah electricity bills rising? Energy company says aircon use likeliest reason as tariff unchanged
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 2 — Sabah Electricity has acknowledged growing public concern over higher electricity bills in recent weeks, following claims on social media questioning whether the increase was due to higher tariff rates. He stressed that the increase in electricity bills is largely driven by higher energy consumption rather than any changes in tariff rates. “The rise in electricity bills is influenced by several key factors, including increased usage due to prolonged hot weather, which has led to more frequent use of cooling appliances such as air conditioners,” he said in a statement issued on Saturday after attending the Sabah Electricity Kaamatan Chess Festival 2026 on Friday.

items.title: Negri Sembilan Umno: 'We'd rather be opposition than stay in PH-led govt'
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Negri Sembilan Umno said it would rather sit as the opposition than remain in the state government following its decision to withdraw support for Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun. State chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias said the party did not want to be tainted by alleged wrongdoing involving the menteri besar in relation to the state's adat and constitutional crisis. Negri Sembilan has 36 state seats.

items.title: UK PM mulls banning some pro-Palestinian marches
items.summary: LONDON: Britain's Prime Minister Keir Starmer said in an interview broadcast on Saturday that banning some pro-Palestinian marches could be justified, especially when they call for the intifada to spread. Labour leader Starmer is under pressure to act after a spate of antisemitic incidents, including this week, when two men were stabbed in the north London suburb of Golders Green, which is home to a large Jewish community. The prime minister, a former human rights lawyer and chief public prosecutor whose wife is of Jewish origin, said many Jewish people had told them they were affected by "the repeat nature" of the protests.

items.title: Backing the safety net: Malaysians working in Singapore welcome Perkeso protection
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Malaysians working in Singapore say the government's plan to extend Social Security Organisation (Socso) coverage to citizens employed abroad will minimise their exposure to financial risks. Akmal Hakim Zainuddin, 35, who has worked at SingPost for six years, said the move would provide social security in the event of accidents, especially for those who commute to the island republic daily. He said many Malaysians working in Singapore rely solely on employer-provided protection, which varies in terms of benefits and coverage.

items.title: Dzulkefly: No cuts to critical healthcare services, vows counter-proposal
items.summary: SANDAKAN: The Health Ministry has said healthcare services, particularly the supply of medicines and medical devices, should not be affected despite a proposed 10 per cent budget cut for the health sector. Its minister, Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad, said public facilities under the ministry are the backbone of essential healthcare services, with operational needs dependent on government hospitals. For me, matters related to healthcare services, medicine supply and medical devices are almost non-negotiable.

items.title: Immigration swoops on Kuching premises, detains 17 foreigners in crackdown on undocumented workers
items.summary: KUCHING, May 2 — The Immigration Department (JIM) in Sarawak arrested 17 foreigners in an operation conducted around the city yesterday. JIM Sarawak, in a statement, announced that of the total, 16 individuals were Indonesians, while the others were Pakistani nationals. “All those detained were found to have failed to produce valid passports or travel documents during inspections carried out at several premises, including shared houses and rented rooms,” said JIM Sarawak.

items.title: Abang Johari leads Sarawak delegation to UK, Ireland in push for AI, data centres and semiconductor growth
items.summary: KUCHING, May 2 — Sarawak Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg is leading an official working visit to Manchester and Bristol, United Kingdom (UK), and Dublin, Ireland, starting today until May 5. “In Dublin, Abang Johari is scheduled to visit the K2 Data Centre, while in Manchester, he will participate in the Sarawak Agenda-Townhall programme in addition to attending a dinner and gathering with Sarawakians. “In addition, Abang Johari will also hold an engagement session with representatives of the Manchester Regional Government at the British Muslim Heritage Centre,” said UKAS.

items.title: Four teens arrested for assaulting boy, dunking head in bucket
items.summary: MELAKA: Police have detained four secondary school students for allegedly bullying a 12-year-old boy at an orphanage in Pantai Puteri, Tanjung Kling. Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said the victim was reportedly kicked and punched by a group of senior students. Acting on the report, police arrested four boys, aged 15 and 16, the following day.

items.title: After withdrawing support, Negeri Sembilan Umno says MB’s fate now with BN, PH leadership
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Negeri Sembilan Umno will defer to party leadership on its next course of action following its withdrawal of support for the menteri besar, including any decision on a potential replacement, its chief Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias said today. Jalaluddin said the state chapter would abide by decisions made by Barisan Nasional (BN) and Pakatan Harapan (PH) leadership, including whether the next menteri besar should come from outside Umno. This concerns governance and the institutions of adat and the Negeri Sembilan monarchy,” he said.

items.title: Two arrested after police chase ends in crash in Subang Jaya [WATCH]
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: Two people were arrested after a police chase involving a Proton Preve ended in a crash with a BMW at a traffic light junction in Subang Jaya last night. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Shamsudin Mamat said police received a report on the incident at 9.05pm on May 1. He said the suspects were arrested by police at the scene.

items.title: Trump says US Navy acting 'like pirates' to carry out naval blockade of Iranian ports
items.summary: WASHINGTON: President Donald Trump said on Friday the US Navy was acting "like pirates" in carrying out Washington's naval blockade of Iranian ports during the US and Israel's war against Iran. "We're like pirates. The US and Israel attacked Iran on February 28.

items.title: Woman, 62, claims loan sharks assaulted her in restaurant over debt
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 62-year-old woman has alleged she was assaulted by loan sharks inside a restaurant owned by them over unpaid debts. During a press conference held alongside the Malaysian Humanitarian Organisation (MHO), the victim, who wishes to be known as Ms Wong, said she had taken out loans to cover medical expenses for her husband, who is battling cancer. Recounting the ordeal, she said that inside a restaurant operated by the alleged loan shark, a young man pulled down the shutters before he and his associates began punching and kicking her.

items.title: Jealous rage likely motive behind Ketereh college girl’s killing, Kelantan police say amid week-long remand for boyfriend and buddy
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 2 — Jealousy is believed to be the motive behind the murder of a 19-year-old college student whose body was found with 61 stab wounds in Keterah yesterday. Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said investigators suspect the victim was stabbed to death by her 19-year-old boyfriend, who allegedly believed she was having an affair, the New Straits Times reported today. He said the boyfriend, identified as the main suspect, was arrested yesterday alongside another youth believed to be a friend of the victim.

items.title: [UPDATED] No backing down: 14 Negri Sembilan Umno reps firm on withdrawal of support for MB [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Negri Sembilan Umno chief Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias said the 14 assemblymen from the party who withdrew confidence from Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun will not be reversing their position. Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said on Thursday that all 14 assemblymen had agreed to continue supporting the unity government in the state. On whether Umno would support a new Menteri Besar candidate who is also from Pakatan Harapan (PH), Jalaluddin said that decision was not for Umno Negri Sembilan to make.

items.title: US sanctions are 'collective punishment,' says Cuba during May 1 marches
items.summary: HAVANA: Cuba said Friday that US President Donald Trump's fresh sanctions on the island amounted to "collective punishment," as an enormous May 1 procession outside the American embassy in Havana vowed to "defend the homeland." In an executive order on Friday, the US leader said he would impose sanctions on people involved in sweeping sections of the Cuban economy, which is steered by the government. The latest sanctions constitute "collective punishment" of the nation's people, Cuban Foreign Minister Bruno Rodriguez said.

items.title: Two new rabies cases detected in Sarawak
items.summary: KUCHING: Two new rabies-positive cases have been recorded in Kuching, bringing the cumulative total to 14 cases detected in Sarawak this year. The Sarawak Veterinary Services Department (JPVS), in a statement on Facebook, said one case was detected in Sungai Tengah Kolong 2 involving a free-roaming pet dog. Another case was detected in Kampung Sungai Atas Singai, Bau, involving a dog bite incident involving a free-roaming pet dog.

items.title: Canada approves new generic Semaglutide
items.summary: OTTAWA, (Canada): Health Canada on Friday authorised a second generic version of the popular diabetes drug semaglutide, further solidifying the country's position as the first G7 nation to approve alternatives to the brand-name drug Ozempic, reported Xinhua. The authorisation followed a "thorough review" demonstrating the drug meets Health Canada's criteria for safety, efficacy and quality, said the release. The move came just three days after Health Canada became the first regulator in a G7 country to approve a generic semaglutide filed by India's Dr. Reddy's Laboratories.

items.title: 'Trump is an idiot': Californians fume over soaring petrol prices
items.summary: LOS ANGELES: At a gas station in Los Angeles, Ryder Thomas wore a grimace of barely suppressed anger as he filled his pickup truck, watching the cost tick up to US$130 for a full tank – US$30 more than he was paying before the US and Israel attacked Iran. This week, pump prices in the United States climbed to their highest level since early 2022, when Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine triggered a global surge in the cost of oil. DOMINO EFFECT The result of this squeeze on supply is rising prices, sending gas in already-expensive California above US$6 a gallon (US$1.59 per liter), compared with around US$4.50 a gallon before the war.

items.title: Poll: 61 pct of citizens say US conflict with Iran a mistake
items.summary: WASHINGTON: A majority of United States (US) citizens believe their country's military use of force against Iran was a mistake, according to a new poll published on Friday, reported Xinhua. Sixty-one per cent of US citizens say that the use of military force against Iran was a mistake, with fewer than two in ten believing that the US actions in Iran have been successful, according to the Washington Post-ABC News-Ipsos poll. In addition, most US citizens believe that the military action against Iran carries potential risks.

items.title: One day, two fatal falls at KLIA2: Police identify second foreigner as 30-year-old Algerian man
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 2 — Police have confirmed another fatal incident of a foreigner after falling at Kuala Lumpur International Airport Terminal 2 (KLIA T2), last night. KLIA district police chief ACP M. Ravi said in the incident at about 9.10 pm, a 30-year-old Algerian man is believed to have fallen from the outside of the balcony at the terminal. Yesterday afternoon, police reported that a woman from China fell to her death from Level 3 of Kuala Lumpur International Airport Terminal 2.

items.title: Regular play spot but not her slippers, says mum of missing nine-year-old girl swept into Bandar Mahkota Cheras drain
items.summary: KAJANG, May 2 — A nine-year-old girl, feared drowned after falling into a drain and swept away by strong currents in Bandar Mahkota Cheras yesterday, often played in the area where the incident occurred. Day Two of a search and rescue for a nine-year-old girl who was swept away by strong currents in Bandar Mahkota Cheras, Selangor on May 1, 2026. A search and rescue for a nine-year-old girl who was swept away by strong currents in Bandar Mahkota Cheras, Selangor on May 1, 2026, expands into the underground drainage system on Day Two.— Bernama pic Media reports yesterday cited that a nine-year-old girl fell into a drain while chasing a ball, before being swept away by strong currents.

items.title: DBKL imposes 300m buffer zone on Bukit Kiara project after TTDI residents’ concerns, says Hannah Yeoh
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) has imposed a 300m buffer zone on a proposed development opposite the Taman Persekutuan Bukit Kiara recreational park, following complaints from residents in Taman Tun Dr Ismail (TTDI). “Kuala Lumpur is a city, and its people need green spaces for recreational activities and to de-stress,” she said. Yeoh spoke to the media after an event at the park today, organised by local non-profit Friends of Bukit Kiara (FoBK), where Kuala Lumpur mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud and FoBK president Leon Koay were also present.

items.title: Funeral in Kuala Kangsar for murdered college student, 19; grieving family asks for privacy
items.summary: KUALA KANGSAR, May 2 — The family of a 19-year-old student found stabbed to death 61 times in Kampung Simah, Ketereh, Kelantan, asks for space to grieve in peace. A visit to the victim’s family home in Kampung Batu Jarau here revealed a solemn silence, with only her closest relatives present. She was buried at 5.30am May 2 at Kampung Periang Muslim Cemetery in Kuala Kangsar, Perak.

items.title: Second-day search underway for nine-year-old feared swept away in Bandar Mahkota Cheras drain
items.summary: KAJANG, May 2 — The search and rescue (SAR) operation for a nine-year-old girl feared swept away after falling into a drain in Bandar Mahkota Cheras while retrieving a ball has entered its second day, with search efforts expanded to a five-kilometre radius. According to Sinar Harian , Selangor Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) operations assistant director Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said the operation involves firefighters from the Kajang station, supported by two Water Rescue Team (PPDA) units from Shah Alam and KLIA. Ahmad Mukhlis said the search team is also using IWK’s pole camera to assist in scanning inside the sewer system.

items.title: Umno must reinvent itself or risk irrelevance, says Mohamad Hasan as party marks 80th anniversary
items.summary: Speaking at Umno’s 80th anniversary gathering — which coincided with his 70th birthday — the Rembau MP said the party could no longer rely on legacy or nostalgia to stay relevant. Some even say they no longer need Umno,” he said. “However, Umno is not dead.

items.title: Pahang man, 65, dies in Mecca after sudden collapse before Friday prayers at Masjidil Haram
items.summary: MECCA, May 2 — A Malaysian haj pilgrim died after collapsing while on his way to perform Friday prayers at the courtyard of the Grand Mosque here yesterday. Malaysian Haj Delegation head Mohd Hisham Harun said the 65-year-old male pilgrim from Pahang was pronounced dead at 12.15 pm local time (5.15 pm Malaysian time) due to heart complications. Mohd Hisham said the deceased, who had travelled to the Holy Land with his wife, was at the Grand Mosque courtyard to perform Friday prayers when he suddenly collapsed and lost consciousness.

items.title: Umno sees membership surge as online registration dubbed ‘gamechanger’, says Asyraf Wajdi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Umno secretary-general Datuk Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki today said the party has recorded an extraordinary surge in new memberships, crediting a “gamechanger” move by the party president for enabling online registration. Asyraf said that in a break from tradition, this year’s party celebration is not an exclusive event. The breakdown showed Umno Youth recording 8,233 new members, followed by 1,260 from Wanita Umno and 1,240 from Puteri Umno.

items.title: Ketereh murder probe widens: Four suspects now held over college student found with 61 stab wounds
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 2 — Police have detained two more suspects to help with the probe into the discovery of a 19-year-old woman’s body with 61 stab wounds near paddy fields in Kampung Simah, Ketereh, early yesterday morning. Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said a team from the Kelantan Contingent Police Headquarters’ Criminal Investigation Department, working with Kota Bharu district police headquarters, launched a raid from 4.40 pm yesterday until 2.30 am today. He said the raid targeted two separate locations, Taman Murni on Jalan Abdul Kadir Adabi and Kampung Belukar Luas in Ketereh.

items.title: KL must stay open and modern, not be held back by ‘reckless narratives’, says Kepong MP on Rain Rave festival
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — The Rain Rave Water Music Festival 2026 is a lawful and well-organised event that has generated real economic activity for local traders, workers and businesses, said Kepong MP Lim Lip Eng. In a statement today, Lim said the event had attracted large crowds and contributed positively to local economic activity, but noted that some parties were attempting to “manufacture controversy out of nothing”. He said such “empty politics” would only damage public confidence and undermine efforts to grow Malaysia’s tourism sector.

items.title: Video misidentified tiger at Osaka zoo, not Malayan tiger, says Perhilitan
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 2 — The Department of Wildlife and National Parks (Perhilitan) today refuted claims that a purported Malayan Tiger was spotted in a weak and sickly condition at Tennoji Zoo in Osaka, Japan, as seen in a viral video circulating on social media. In a statement issued today, Perhilitan clarified that the animal in the video is an Amur Tiger (Panthera tigris altaica), not a Malayan Tiger (Panthera tigris jacksoni). “This has sparked public concern and negative reactions, particularly regarding wildlife welfare and the perceived image of Malayan Tiger conservation,” the statement read.

items.title: Songkran is cultural heritage, not water festival entertainment, says Malaysian Siam Association amid growing confusion
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — Persatuan Siam Malaysia (PSM) president Sam Maark Suvanphet yesterday said that there is confusion between the Songkran celebration and water-based entertainment events such as water festivals or water music festivals. He said Songkran is the Siamese New Year celebration, which carries cultural, family and religious values, including traditional practices such as seeking blessings from parents and performing religious activities at temples. In contrast, he said water music festivals and similar events held at certain locations are entertainment and economic programmes with no cultural or religious link to the Siamese community.

items.title: Police investigate KLIA2 fall death involving 27-year-old woman scheduled to fly home to China
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 2 — Police have confirmed that a Chinese national died after falling from Level 3 of the Kuala Lumpur International Airport Terminal 2 (KLIA Terminal 2) in an incident that went viral on social media yesterday. KLIA Police Chief ACP M. Ravi, in a statement today, said the incident occurred at 2.20 pm. Those with information regarding the incident are urged to contact the KLIA District Police Headquarters at 03-8776 8222 to assist in the investigation.

items.title: Perak family learns of college student’s brutal killing a week after Kelantan campus drop-off, brother says couldn’t bear to hear details
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 2 — The family of a 19-year-old college student found with 61 stab wounds in Kampung Simah, Ketereh, yesterday morning is struggling to come to terms with her loss after sending her back to campus last week. The victim’s brother, Mohd Faris Zulkifly, 21, said the family had travelled to Kelantan on Saturday to send his sister, Nurfisya Zulkifly, 19, for the new semester. “Without delay, we left Kuala Kangsar, Perak, for Kelantan,” he told reporters at the Forensic Unit of Hospital Raja Perempuan Zainab II (HRPZ II) last night.

items.title: Two men from Tajikistan and Palestine held after begging claims at KLIA go viral
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 2 — Police arrested two foreign men early yesterday morning, suspected of being involved in begging activities at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) Terminal. KLIA Police Chief ACP M. Ravi said the suspects, aged 28 and 45, are believed to be the individuals shown in a viral social media video of begging at the terminal. “The suspects’ modus operandi was to approach visitors at the airport to ask for money by eliciting sympathy,” he said in a statement yesterday.

items.title: After royal crisis, Negeri Sembilan Ruler thanks supporters amid show of solidarity at mosque
items.summary: JEMPOL, May 2 — The Yang Dipertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, yesterday expressed his appreciation for the support shown by various parties. His Royal Highness conveyed his gratitude when meeting supporters at the compound of the Tuanku Muhriz Mosque in Bandar Seri Jempol. Earlier, Tuanku Muhriz, accompanied by his sons, Tunku Besar Seri Menanti Tunku Ali Redhauddin and Tunku Zain Al-’Abidin, performed solat hajat with about 1,500 congregants at the mosque.

items.title: Yeo Bee Yin backs Rain Rave festival, says ‘moral policing’ hurts tourism push
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Former minister Yeo Bee Yin has defended the Rain Rave Water Music Festival (RRWMF) amid ongoing backlash, saying critics should not overreact and warned that excessive “moral policing” could harm Malaysia’s tourism sector. “We must remember that Malaysia’s strength lies in our diversity, and that includes offering a variety of events that appeal to a global audience — especially during Visit Malaysia Year,” the Puchong MP said. Taking aim at critics of the Rain Rave Water Music Festival, she said the backlash was disproportionate.

items.title: Gaza aid mission turns violent: Ten Malaysians in flotilla freed in Greece after claims of attack in custody
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Ten Malaysian delegates from the Sumud Nusantara Command Center (SNCC), who were detained in international waters near the Greek island of Crete on Thursday, have been released at the port of Lerapetra in Crete. SNCC director-general Datuk Dr Sani Araby Abdul Alim Araby said the ten were among 175 humanitarian activists from the Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) transported to Heraklion Airport in buses provided by the Greek authorities. Sani Araby said that SNCC received reports that activists faced provocation and physical attacks during their detention.

items.title: Kelantan police confirm woman found dead with 61 stab wounds near paddy field is missing college student aged 19
items.summary: Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said the victim was a semester three student at a local college. Speaking to Bernama last night, he said police are actively tracking down suspects involved in the case. — Bernama
